steps.title: Identify the dependent clause needing introduction
steps.content: After 'pattern [noun]' comes 'biologists believe can be used to explain...'. This clause cannot stand alone—it depends on being connected to the noun to explain what that noun is or does.

steps.title: Apply the rule for introducing a dependent clause
steps.content: A clause that modifies a noun is introduced by 'that' with no comma before 'that'. Option C 'formation that some' correctly uses 'that' to connect noun and clause. The others either separate the noun from its clause with punctuation (A, B) or incorrectly add a comma before 'that' (D). That matches choice C — C is correct.
